title: lathyrus palustris subsp. palustris
intro: Lathyrus palustris subsp. palustris, also known as marsh pea or water pea, is a subspecies of the Lathyrus palustris plant, a type of flowering legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lathyrus palustris subsp. palustris include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
